sources.comparison_caveat: User accuracy depends on geometry, blockage, atmosphere, multipath, and receiver quality; URE is not user position accuracy.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Open-sky GNSS, broadcast GNSS signals, GNSS control segment, receiver solution for position and time.
sources.label: standalone_gnss_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Smartphone open-sky example 4.9 m radius; high-quality single-frequency FAA data <=1.82 m horizontal, 95%; GPS URE <=2.0 m, 95%.
sources.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Accuracy

sources.comparison_caveat: Service commitment/global metric, not a particular UE, urban, or NTN receiver result.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Civil GPS SPS, healthy constellation, global service definitions, GPS control segment.
sources.label: standalone_gnss_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: 2024 SPS metrics include <=8 m horizontal 95% global average, <=13 m vertical 95% global average, and <=30 ns time transfer 95%.
sources.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Performance

sources.comparison_caveat: Not a generic mobile UE clock guarantee.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Specialized time-transfer receiver, fixed site, GNSS time infrastructure.
sources.label: standalone_gnss_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: GPS time-transfer accuracy relative to UTC(USNO) <=30 ns, 95%, for specialized fixed time-transfer receivers.
sources.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Accuracy - timing

sources.comparison_caveat: Standards support and architecture, not an accuracy guarantee.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: NG-RAN and LMF positioning architecture.
sources.label: gnss_aided_initialization
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: NG-RAN positioning supports A-GNSS along with NR positioning methods such as DL-TDOA, DL-AoD, Multi-RTT, UL-TDOA, and UL-AoA.
sources.source_title: 3GPP TS 38.305 UE positioning in NG-RAN

sources.comparison_caveat: Protocol support is not a standalone accuracy claim.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Location server such as E-SMLC, LMF, or SLP; cellular control/user-plane assistance.
sources.label: gnss_aided_initialization
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: LPP covers E-UTRA and NR positioning and includes A-GNSS assistance/provide-location procedures.
sources.source_title: 3GPP TS 37.355 LTE Positioning Protocol (LPP)

sources.comparison_caveat: Defines assistance architecture/test cases, not a universal accuracy number.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: SUPL-enabled terminal, SUPL location platform/server, IP/user-plane connectivity.
sources.label: gnss_aided_initialization
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: SUPL test cases include A-GPS/A-GNSS SET-assisted and SET-based modes.
sources.source_title: OMA SUPL v2.0 enabler test specification

sources.comparison_caveat: Aviation augmentation infrastructure; not unaided GNSS or GNSS-denied JCLS.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: WAAS reference stations, master stations, GEO broadcast, and aviation receiver modes.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: FAA monitored WAAS reports provide site-level horizontal/vertical 95% performance statistics under aviation integrity modes.
sources.source_title: FAA WAAS Performance Analysis Report

sources.comparison_caveat: Precision and absolute accuracy differ; requires correction infrastructure and favorable GNSS conditions.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Known base station, rover, correction data link, calibration, and field quality control.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Survey-grade real-time precision often cited around 1 cm + 1 ppm horizontal and 2 cm + 1 ppm vertical at 1 sigma, when procedures and ambiguity fixing are successful.
sources.source_title: NOAA NGS User Guidelines for Single Base Real Time GNSS Positioning

sources.comparison_caveat: Network correction infrastructure is not comparable to GNSS-denied cooperative JCLS.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: CORS/RTN network, datum alignment, cellular/internet data link, and user QC.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: RTN guidelines discuss centimeter-class horizontal/vertical precision under controlled network and field procedures.
sources.source_title: NOAA NGS Guidelines for Real Time GNSS Networks

sources.comparison_caveat: Postprocessed survey infrastructure, not real-time standalone GNSS or JCLS.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Reference-station network, code/carrier data, postprocessing, and NSRS alignment.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: CORS supports postprocessed high-accuracy positioning workflows through fiducial reference stations.
sources.source_title: NOAA CORS Network

sources.comparison_caveat: High-infrastructure reference, not a fair GNSS-denied baseline.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Reference stations, correction streams, satellite/internet delivery, or commercial correction service.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: RTK, DGNSS, SBAS, and PPP are correction methods; RTK uses correction links, PPP can provide centimetre-level accuracy with global corrections.
sources.source_title: NovAtel: RTK vs PPP correction services

sources.comparison_caveat: Correction products are external infrastructure and may have latency or service constraints.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Global GNSS monitoring and analysis-center infrastructure.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: IGS provides precise orbit and clock products; PPP-AR uses precise products and bias information.
sources.source_title: International GNSS Service products / PPP-AR

sources.comparison_caveat: Correction-stream performance is not direct user position accuracy and assumes external infrastructure.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Real-time global tracking, analysis centers, correction streams, and PPP receiver processing.
sources.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: IGS real-time correction literature reports centimeter-class orbit and sub-nanosecond clock correction targets/behavior for PPP inputs.
sources.source_title: Performance of real-time IGS satellite clocks for PPP

sources.comparison_caveat: Literature context only; assumptions differ from JCLS cooperative NTN.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Signal-specific receiver, TLE/orbit information, batching/EKF, sometimes altitude or other aiding.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Survey reports examples including 7.7 m 2D Starlink with altitude aiding, 25.9 m 2D and 33.5 m 3D without external altitude over 800 s using six satellites.
sources.source_title: Receiver architectures for positioning with low earth orbit satellite signals: a survey

sources.comparison_caveat: Proof-of-concept experiment, not an operational PNT service or infrastructure-matched JCLS baseline.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Passive/non-cooperative Starlink signal exploitation, carrier-phase tracking, static receiver, and aiding for best case.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Reported experimental examples include 7.7 m 2D error with known altitude and tens-of-meters 2D/3D errors without external altitude over an extended batch.
sources.source_title: The First Carrier Phase Tracking and Positioning Results With Starlink LEO Satellite Signals

sources.comparison_caveat: Observability context, not a general accuracy benchmark.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Known LEO satellite state over time and pseudorange extraction from LEO signals.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Analyzes observability from LEO pseudorange measurements over time and includes experimental demonstrations.
sources.source_title: Observability Analysis of Opportunistic Receiver Localization with LEO Satellite Pseudorange Measurements

sources.comparison_caveat: Design-study context, not a reproduced baseline for this manuscript.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: NR-NTN physical-layer enhancements, PRS resource design, LEO orbit/signal simulation framework.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Discusses LEO-based NR-NTN positioning as complementary infrastructure to GNSS and potentially an alternative with enhancements.
sources.source_title: LEO-based Positioning: Foundations, Signal Design, and Receiver Enhancements for 6G NTN

sources.comparison_caveat: Useful for framing weak/unavailable GNSS, not for direct numerical comparison.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: 3GPP-related NTN positioning assumptions and CRLB simulation study.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Identifies multi-LEO positioning and GNSS-augmented LEO positioning when insufficient GNSS satellites are visible.
sources.source_title: NTN-based 6G Localization: Vision, Role of LEOs, and Open Problems

sources.comparison_caveat: LEO-augmented GNSS, not standalone cooperative NTN JCLS.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: GNSS PPP infrastructure plus simulated/augmented LEO observations.
sources.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Reports LEO-augmented GNSS PPP geometry/accuracy improvements under the paper's simulation and data assumptions.
sources.source_title: LEO enhanced GNSS precise point positioning with emphasis on model comparison

sources.comparison_caveat: The sweep values are scenario assumptions, not final literature-calibrated device models.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: Local oscillator holdover after GNSS loss.
sources.label: intermittent_gnss_update
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: TCXO frequency stability options span roughly +/-0.05 to +/-1.5 ppm.
sources.source_title: Rakon TCXO product family

sources.comparison_caveat: Application-specific timing holdover, not UE-grade clock-drift model.
sources.infrastructure_assumptions: High-quality oscillator holdover and GPS timing application.
sources.label: intermittent_gnss_update
sources.reported_or_relevant_value: Reports maintaining timing within 1 us drift for more than 1.5 hours in a GPS holdover context.
sources.source_title: PMU holdover performance enhancement using double-oven controlled oscillator
